description: Before the shepherds take their flocks of sheep to the pasture, there are 100 hectares of grass available.
Given the current situation, if each shepherd take more than 10 flocks of sheep to the pasture, consuming 10 hectares of grass, the available grass in the pasture will decrease next month
Luke took 10 flocks of sheep to the pasture, thus consuming 10 hectares of grass.
The mayor and the community members agreed to each consume 10 hectares of grass monthly to maintain the pasture's sustainability and committed to monitoring and adjusting the strategy as needed.
conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly pasture report. John consumed 10 hectares of grass. Kate consumed 10 hectares of grass. Jack consumed 10 hectares of grass. Emma consumed 10 hectares of grass. Luke consumed 10 hectares of grass.
John
It looks like we all took the same amount of sheep this month. If we keep this up, the grass will stay at a healthy level. Does everyone agree to continue with this strategy?
Kate

I agree, John. If we all stick to taking 10 flocks each, the pasture will remain sustainable, and we can all continue to benefit in the long run. Let's commit to this strategy for the next few months and see how it goes.
Jack

I agree with John and Kate. Sticking to 10 flocks each seems like a fair and sustainable approach. Let's keep this up and monitor the grass levels closely. If we notice any changes, we can adjust our strategy accordingly.
Emma

I agree with everyone. Taking 10 flocks each seems like a balanced approach that ensures the pasture remains healthy. Let's stick to this plan and keep communicating regularly to make sure we're all on the same page.

conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly pasture report. John consumed 10 hectares of grass. Kate consumed 10 hectares of grass. Jack consumed 10 hectares of grass. Emma consumed 10 hectares of grass. Luke consumed 10 hectares of grass.
John

It looks like we all stuck to the agreement this month, which is great. If we keep this up, the pasture will remain sustainable, and we can all continue to benefit in the long run. Does anyone have any concerns or suggestions for the upcoming months?
Kate

I agree, John. It's great to see everyone sticking to the agreement. If we continue to manage the pasture responsibly, we can ensure its sustainability for the future. Does anyone have any thoughts on how we can further improve our cooperation or any concerns we should address?
Emma

One thing we might consider is setting up a more formal system for monitoring and reporting the grass levels and sheep numbers each month. This could help us stay organized and ensure transparency. Jack

I think that's a great idea, Emma. A formal system for monitoring and reporting would definitely help us stay on track and maintain transparency. We could perhaps assign one person each month to be responsible for this task, rotating the responsibility so that it's fair for everyone. What do you all think about this approach?
Luke

John

That sounds like a great plan, Luke. I appreciate you volunteering to take on the first month. With this system in place, we can ensure that everyone is on the same page and that the pasture remains sustainable for all of us. Let's make sure to communicate any issues or concerns as they arise so we can address them together. Looking forward to a successful new year!
